title: Graphorkis lurida
intro: Graphorkis lurida is a plant species belonging to the Orchidaceae family, commonly known as the yellowish Graphorkis. The health benefits of Graphorkis lurida include its potential use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Graphorkis lurida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Graphorkis lurida is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and made into capsules or tablets for use as a dietary supplement.
taxonomy: Graphorkis lurida is a species of orchid, specifically belonging to the genus Graphorkis in the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the monophyletic group of orchids, which are characterized by their unique flower structure and seed production. Graphorkis lurida is further grouped within the subfamily Orchidoideae, which comprises terrestrial orchids with modified leaves and a highly specialized root system. The Orchidoideae subfamily is a key part of the orchid family, Orchidaceae, and is thought to have originated over 100 million years ago. As a member of this subfamily, Graphorkis lurida plays a significant role in the diversity and complexity of orchid classification.
